attributes.description: Fires when every logical CPU is heavily utilised in the lookback window. Uses idle utilisation: non-idle share per core is 1.0 minus the idle state ratio, then the coolest core in the window must still exceed 90% non-idle. Tune 0.9 in the ESQL to match your SLO. Requires 2 consecutive positive evaluations to reduce flapping from short spikes.
attributes.name: [System Host Metrics OTel] CPU sustained high on all cores

investigation_guide.blob:
## CPU high on all cores

1. In Dashboards, open the host **CPU** view and check per-`cpu` and `state` breakdown.
2. Compare `system.cpu.load_average.5m` to `system.cpu.logical.count`.
3. Identify top processes (outside this dataset if process scraper is enabled).

**Tune** Lower `0.9` in the ESQL to alert earlier (e.g. 0.85).

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-hostmetricsreceiver.otel-*
// Idle state util is idle fraction; non-idle share approximates 1 - idle for that row.
| WHERE `attributes.state` == "idle"
| WHERE `metrics.system.cpu.utilization` IS NOT NULL
| EVAL non_idle = 1.0 - `metrics.system.cpu.utilization`
| STATS per_core_peak = MAX(non_idle) BY `host.name`, `attributes.cpu`
// Coolest core in the window: if its peak non-idle is still > threshold, all cores are hot.
| STATS min_coolest_core = MIN(per_core_peak) BY `host.name`
| WHERE min_coolest_core > 0.9
| SORT min_coolest_core DESC
| LIMIT 20
